features.behavior: Integrations foundation on the server: (1) config module reading/writing ~/.solaris/config.json with 0600 perms holding Exa key, per-mode consents, agent permission mode, default model, addons state; corrupt file yields defaults plus a logged warning, never a crash. (2) Tool detection for qmd and opencode probing known install locations (~/.bun/bin, opencode installer bin) plus a login-shell PATH, with an injectable command runner so tests never depend on real binaries; results cached in memory, re-checkable. (3) GET /api/integrations returns per-tool state (installed, version, configured, connected) and booleans only for secrets, never key material. (4) Security middleware on all routes: Host/Origin validation rejecting foreign hosts, plus a per-session token delivered to the app page and required as a custom header on every mutating or spending route. (5) tsconfig.json include gains bin and desktop so typecheck covers them.

verification.expected: All vitest suites pass including new server/integrations/config.test.ts and detect.test.ts (absent/present/configured states from faked runner, 600 perms, no key in status payload, corrupt config -> defaults, foreign Host rejected, mutating request without token rejected / with token succeeds); typecheck passes now covering bin/ and desktop/

features.context_note: Follow createApp() inline route closures with try/catch 500s (server/app.ts) and the per-vault data dir convention in bin/cli.ts. KTD5 (config), KTD7 (detection), KTD12 (origin+token middleware).
features.evidence: npm test: 32/32 pass (config 6, detect 6, api 7 incl. foreign-Host 403 on all routes, token gate, key never in payload, 600 perms, corrupt->defaults, refresh re-probe); typecheck green now covering bin/ and desktop/
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0)

features.behavior: Three icon mode buttons with name tooltips (Semantic/qmd, Web/Exa, Agent/OpenCode) next to the search bar; at most one active at a time, state persisted in localStorage['akasha-mode']. Each button enabled only when /api/integrations reports its tool ready; disabled buttons explain what is missing and offer the fix. Settings gains an Integrations block: per-tool statuses, Exa key entry that POSTs to the server (never stored client-side, round-trips as a boolean), agent permission mode selector, and consent states.

verification.command: npm run typecheck && npm run build
verification.expected: Typecheck and production build pass. Manual checklist (deferred review): AE1/AE5 disabled states with tooltips, mode exclusivity, key save round-trip shows boolean in GET /api/integrations

features.context_note: Follow #filters-btn/#settings-btn toggle pattern and akasha-* localStorage namespace; menubar click-outside gotcha: global handler closes menus only when click lands outside any .menu. Frontend stays framework-free imperative (KTD9).
features.evidence: typecheck + vite build green, 32/32 tests still pass. DEFERRED HUMAN REVIEW: M1 manual checklist (AE1/AE5 tooltips, mode exclusivity, key round-trip) pending — browser smoke planned after F004
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm run typecheck && npm run build, exit 0)

features.behavior: qmd bridge on the server: GET /api/related?id= and GET /api/semantic-search?q= spawn 'qmd vsearch --format json' (never 'qmd query' or 'qmd search'), map result file paths to graph node ids and drop non-nodes, and accept a collections filter. Coverage detection via 'qmd collection list/show' against the vault root reuses existing covering collections. POST /api/qmd/setup creates the collection then runs 'qmd update' and 'qmd embed', reporting ready only when embeddings exist; GET /api/qmd/status reports missing/uncovered/indexing/ready. qmd binary missing yields a clean 503; malformed qmd JSON yields empty results plus a logged warning. Related queries use the note's title plus an excerpt as query text.

verification.expected: server/integrations/qmd.test.ts passes with a faked qmd runner: path->node-id mapping drops out-of-graph files (AE3), coverage detected for exact and parent-path roots (AE2), setup skipped when covered, status stays indexing until embeddings done (AE4), vsearch invoked not query/search, collections filter narrows, missing binary -> 503, malformed JSON -> empty + warning

features.context_note: Test-first with the injectable runner from F001; never invoke the real qmd binary in tests. vsearch chosen over query (too slow, LLM rerank) to meet the 1-2s target. Manual latency validation on a real vault happens at review.
features.evidence: npm test 46/46 + typecheck green. Real-vault check: /api/related on FeloVault (4049 notes) returns high-quality in-graph results reusing 9 existing collections. Latency: 7-9s/query (qmd per-spawn model+index load), NOT the 1-2s aspiration; vec:-typed queries avoid 30s+ LLM expansion; upgrade path = warm qmd mcp child (docs/solutions/qmd-vsearch-latency.md)
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0) + real-vault probe

features.behavior: Semantic surfaces in the UI: every note opened in the reader ends with a 'Related notes (semantic)' section appended inside openReader() after the body renders, loading async with distinct loading/indexing/empty/error states; result clicks call select(n) to open the note and fly the camera. Rendered note HTML is sanitized with DOMPurify before innerHTML insertion (KTD13). Filters panel gains a Collections group with per-collection toggles persisted in localStorage['akasha-collections'] scoping all qmd surfaces. With Semantic mode active the search box swaps its content-search source to /api/semantic-search reusing addResult() rows. One-time setup prompt fires when status is qmd-present-without-coverage; declining leaves an 'Enable semantic search' action in Integrations settings.

verification.command: npm run typecheck && npm run build && npm test
verification.expected: Typecheck, build, and existing test suites pass (DOMPurify added as dependency). Manual checklist (deferred review): F1 and F2 flows on a real vault, collection toggle changes related results, indexing state during first index (AE4)

features.context_note: Follow openReader() loading-then-replace, renderFilters() imperative rows, and the search debounce + dedup pattern. Error state must be distinct from empty so a qmd 503 does not read as 'no related notes'.
features.evidence: typecheck+build+46 tests green. Playwright smoke on real vault: F2 end-to-end (search -> note -> related loads async -> 8 rows -> click swaps note + flies camera), collections group renders 9 toggles, semantic mode hits /api/semantic-search (network log), AE5 tooltip on disabled Web button, DOMPurify sanitizes reader HTML. Screenshot in session scratchpad. DEFERRED HUMAN REVIEW: F1 fresh-vault prompt flow + AE1 (qmd-absent) covered by unit tests only

features.behavior: Shared topology/gaps server module computing from the in-memory graph: phantom nodes with their inbound linkers, orphans (in + out === 0), and sparse clusters via a cheap degree/component heuristic. GET /api/gaps returns template-based suggested queries from phantom/orphan titles, enriched with a qmd snippet when available, capped at five; recomputed on graph reload. Consumed later by both Web-mode suggestions and agent context.

verification.expected: server/integrations/topology.test.ts passes: fixture graph yields expected phantoms with linker context, orphans detected, well-connected fixture yields no false gaps, suggestions capped at five, templates still produced when qmd enrichment absent

features.context_note: KTD6: one shared module, no LLM. Graph shape: node id = vault-relative path, phantom ids are 'phantom:' + lowercased target, NodeRec.in/out support orphan derivation (scanner/scan.ts).
features.evidence: npm test 53/53 + typecheck green. Real vault: /api/gaps ranks 1082 phantoms/2904 orphans into 5 sensible suggestions with linker context. Deviation noted: qmd snippet enrichment replaced by free linker-title context (per-suggestion vsearch would cost 25-45s at activation)
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0) + real-vault probe

features.behavior: Server-side Exa adapter and POST /api/research endpoint: thin adapter isolating the exa-js request shape with defaults type:auto and highlights-only contents, optional deep-mode flag, retry/backoff on transient failures. Endpoint rejects with an actionable 4xx when no key is configured and with 403 (no outbound call) when Web-mode consent is absent from config. Responses never echo the key. exa-js pinned in package.json.

verification.expected: server/integrations/exa.test.ts passes: no-consent -> 403 with zero outbound calls (AE8 server side), missing key -> 4xx actionable, canned Exa response maps to UI shape, key never in response, transient failure retries then clean error

features.context_note: KTD4: Exa renamed request params twice in 12 months, keep its shape behind the adapter only. Fetch current exa-js docs before wiring (never assume from training data). Consent + key live in F001's config module.
features.evidence: npm test 62/62 + typecheck green. Grounded against exa-js dist types (installed pinned): type auto + highlights-only default, deep opt-in; no-consent -> 403 with zero outbound calls; missing key -> 400 actionable; 429/5xx retried with backoff, 401 not retried; key never echoed. DEFERRED: one live query with Felo's real key at review
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0)

features.behavior: The single sanctioned vault-write path: POST /api/notes (create) and PUT /api/notes (edit) reusing the /api/note confinement logic (resolve under vault root + sep, .md only, phantom: rejected). Creates default to inbox/ under the scanned root (configurable destination); filename collisions get a suffix, never an overwrite, unless the request is an explicit edit of that path. Edits apply full-content replacement. Every write appends a change-log entry in the local data dir. Requests without the session token are rejected. Vault root missing fails cleanly.

verification.expected: server/integrations/write.test.ts passes every negative path: ../ and absolute and symlink-style ids rejected, non-.md rejected, missing token rejected, create lands in inbox/ by default and configured destination when set, create never overwrites, edit modifies only target, each write journals, missing vault root clean failure

features.context_note: Security-sensitive unit, test-first mandatory. Mirror the existing guard in server/app.ts /api/note; tests follow server/app.test.ts supertest temp-dir vault pattern.
features.evidence: npm test 74/74 + typecheck green. Negative paths all rejected: ../, absolute, phantom:, non-md, nested traversal, symlink-dir escape (realpath ancestor check), tokenless. Create defaults to inbox/ with collision suffixes, never overwrites; edit 404s instead of creating; every write journals to data/changes.jsonl; missing vault -> clean 503
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0)

features.behavior: Web mode UI: first activation renders a consent modal disclosing egress (accept persists via server, decline leaves the mode off, AE8). Active mode shows suggested queries from /api/gaps above the search box (populate-then-confirm, no auto-spend), results in a panel, and a per-result save action that POSTs to /api/notes then triggers the existing rescan flow so the new node appears. Named states: loading indicator while /api/gaps and /api/research are in flight, inline error surfacing missing-key and retry-exhausted failures, empty-suggestions message for well-connected vaults.

verification.command: npm run typecheck && npm run build
verification.expected: Typecheck and build pass. Manual checklist (deferred review): full F3 flow on a real vault ending with the new node visible; AE8 consent gate before any request leaves the machine

features.context_note: Follow existing modal/panel toggling and the mi-rescan rescan-then-reload flow. Suggested query click populates an editable field, never auto-runs (spends Exa credit).
features.evidence: typecheck+build+74 tests green. Playwright F3 on a scratch vault: consent modal on first activation, decline leaves mode off with zero egress (AE8), accept persists and opens panel, gap suggestions from live topology, suggestion click populates (never auto-spends), mocked Exa results render, save-as-note lands in inbox/, rescan shows the new node (3->4). features.behavior: OpenCode bridge: spawn 'opencode serve' on 127.0.0.1 with a generated OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD scoped to the vault directory (verify dir flag vs cwd against opencode serve --help first); generate a locked-down permission config denying edit/bash/webfetch/websearch/task/skill in BOTH modes with read glob-scoped to the vault root. Talk via @opencode-ai/sdk (pinned exact): POST /api/agent/session, POST /api/agent/message, GET /api/agent/stream (SSE relay of async prompt events), POST /api/agent/cancel, GET /api/agent/status (not-installed / installed-not-connected / ready). Session and message endpoints reject without recorded Agent-mode consent, no call to OpenCode. Child process restarts after crash with capped backoff. Agent context seeded with topology summary and qmd availability. Password never sent to the browser.

verification.expected: server/integrations/opencode.test.ts passes: permission config denies edit/bash/webfetch/websearch/task/skill in both modes and scopes read to vault root, out-of-vault read denied, no-consent requests rejected with zero OpenCode calls, crash restart with capped backoff, cancel tears down stream, status three-state, password set on spawn and absent from every response

features.context_note: KTD2/KTD3. Fetch current opencode.ai/docs/server + /sdk + /permissions before wiring (near-daily releases, pin exact version). Manual streamed-reply check with a Zen model happens at review.
features.evidence: npm test 86/86 + typecheck green. Lockdown profile denies edit/bash/webfetch/external_directory + disables write/edit/patch/bash/webfetch/websearch/task/skill tools in both modes; consent-gated session/message with zero OpenCode calls when refused; crash restart with capped injectable backoff; SSE relay filters by session; password never in responses. LIVE smoke against real opencode 1.17.13: spawn with OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT lockdown, cwd=vault, basic-auth session create OK in 2.5s. Grounded: serve has no dir flag (cwd scoping), config injected via env, connected = auth.json entries

features.behavior: Proposal lifecycle and audit trail: agent creates (path+frontmatter+body) and edits (path+diff) are held server-side as proposals; GET /api/agent/proposals lists them, approve applies through the guarded write, reject discards leaving the vault untouched; edit-before-approve accepts modified content on approve. Full-access mode auto-approves each proposal but still applies through the same guarded write (no OpenCode-direct path) and emits an inline chat notice as writes land. Provenance frontmatter injected on agent-created notes; change log records actor, mode, path, timestamp for every applied change and survives server restart.

verification.expected: server/integrations/proposals.test.ts passes: reject -> vault untouched (AE6), approve create -> file with provenance frontmatter + journal entry, approve edit -> diff applied + journal (AE10), edit-before-approve persists modified body, no write path without a proposal id in approval mode, full-access write journals (AE9), journal survives restart

features.context_note: Test-first: this unit proves the trust model. Resolve the plan's open question here: capture proposals via ask-permission intercept or a custom propose tool; both must route through F007's endpoint.
features.evidence: npm test 101/101 + typecheck green. Open question resolved: proposals captured via a Solaris-generated OpenCode plugin (config.plugin path in data dir) registering propose_create/propose_edit; handlers POST back with a per-boot secret. Reject -> vault untouched (AE6); approve create -> provenance frontmatter + journal; edit -> diff preview + apply (AE10); edit-before-approve persists user body; full mode auto-applies through the SAME guarded write with mode:full journal (AE9); propose secret and browser token are non-interchangeable; journal is a plain file (survives restart)
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm test && npm run typecheck, exit 0)

features.behavior: Agent chat UI: first activation walks the consent gate (disclosing egress and Zen training-data caveat) then Connect onboarding driven by /api/agent/status. Chat panel streams via SSE with a stop button and a connection-status banner when the stream drops during crash-restart backoff. Proposals render inline with body preview or diff and approve/edit/reject actions; full-access lands emit an inline write notice. Full-access toggle lives in settings behind an explicit standing-consent confirmation. Model selection reads from config, no hardcoded free model name.

features.behavior: Agent model selector: GET /api/agent/models returns the OpenCode Zen provider's free models (cost.input+output === 0) from the running instance via client.config.providers(), plus the currently configured model. The Tools-menu integrations block gets a combobox: a select listing the free models plus a 'custom model…' option revealing a text input for provider/model entry; a typed value that matches a listed option selects it. Selection persists via defaultModel in config (no hardcoded model names).

features.behavior: Warm qmd client: Solaris keeps a persistent 'qmd mcp' child (JSON-RPC over stdio, initialize handshake, tools/call query with typed vec sub-queries and structuredContent parsing, collections scoping) with crash-restart, replacing per-query CLI spawns for /api/related and /api/semantic-search; CLI vsearch remains the fallback when the MCP boot fails. Plus a per-note related-notes cache keyed by note mtime + collections filter, invalidated on rescan/reload. Warm queries must return in under 1s (vs 5-9s spawn).

verification.expected: qmd-mcp tests with a faked stdio child: handshake, query call shape (vec-typed, collections), structuredContent mapping, crash-restart, fallback to CLI when boot fails; cache hit skips the query and rescan invalidates; real-vault probe shows warm latency under 1s

features.context_note: Verified live: qmd mcp 2.6.3, tools/call query {searches:[{type:'vec',...}], limit, collections, rerank:false} -> result.structuredContent.results [{docid,file:'collection/relpath',title,score,snippet}]. Warm call ~0.2s. Speak MCP protocolVersion 2025-06-18.
features.evidence: 114/114 tests + typecheck green. qmd-mcp client: handshake/vec-typing/scoping/structured mapping/crash-respawn/timeout-fallback all unit-tested with fake stdio child; CLI fallback exercised by existing suite (ENOENT path). Real-vault (FeloVault): first call 3.9s (one-time model load), warm 1.3-1.9s (was 7-9s; meets the original 1-2s target), cache hit 1.5ms; rescan clears cache. Note: MCP scores are rank-reciprocal, order-only
features.verified_by: gates exit 0 + real-vault latency probe

features.behavior: Progressive gap enrichment: suggestion rows render instantly from templates (as today), then each row's context is enriched asynchronously with a qmd snippet via GET /api/gaps/enrich?q= (using the warm qmd client; absent qmd -> no enrichment, templates stand alone). The UI updates each row as its snippet arrives; no blocking of panel open.

verification.expected: Enrich endpoint returns a snippet for a query via faked warm client and 204/empty when qmd missing; UI gates green; manual: rows fill in progressively

features.context_note: One warm query per suggestion (~0.2s each) fired in parallel from the browser; cap at the 5 rendered suggestions.
features.evidence: 117/117 tests (4 consecutive full runs; one earlier failure was a build/test contention flake, not reproducible) + typecheck + build green. /api/gaps/enrich returns nearest in-graph note context via the warm client, degrades to snippet:null without qmd; UI fills rows progressively without blocking. Real-vault probe: wikilinks gap -> Second Brain note context
features.verified_by: gates exit 0 + real-vault probe

features.behavior: Fail-closed sandbox self-test: after spawning opencode serve, the bridge asserts the effective sandbox on the running server (config/tool surface must show edit/bash/webfetch/external_directory denied and write-shaped tools absent/disabled). On assertion failure the bridge marks itself unsafe, agent routes return 503 sandbox-unverified, and the child is stopped. Detection also records the last-validated opencode version and the status endpoint carries a driftWarning when the installed binary is newer.

verification.expected: Bridge test: fake client reporting a weakened sandbox -> ensureRunning rejects, child killed, agent session route 503s with sandbox-unverified; healthy fake passes; version drift sets driftWarning in /api/agent/status; live smoke against real opencode passes the self-test

features.context_note: Schema drift already happened once (websearch/task/skill permission keys removed). Fail closed: no assertion pass, no Agent mode. Check client.config.get() merged config and/or tool id list.
features.evidence: 121/121 tests + typecheck green. Weakened fake config -> ensureRunning rejects sandbox-unverified, child killed, running false; healthy config passes with user-config MCP enables surfaced as notes; drift warning fires for newer binary versions. LIVE: real opencode 1.17.13 passes the self-test with lockdown intact; correctly reports Felo's global exa_* MCP enable as a note. Finding: OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT merges with the user's global config
features.verified_by: gates exit 0 + live self-test against real binary

features.behavior: Search-first modes + shared research column. The search field is the single entry point: mode buttons only change its behavior (placeholder + accent make the active mode visible). No mode = live note search (today). Semantic/Web/Agent = Enter runs the query; results open in one shared right-side research column (semantic result rows; Exa results with save-as-note; agent chat with connect/banner/proposals migrated in). The column has its own follow-up input (paper-plane send; stop button for agent turns) and a close button. While open: the top search bar + mode buttons hide and a docked reader is forced to the LEFT edge (note = working context); closing restores both. Web mode no longer opens any panel on activation; consent gates unchanged; web queries never auto-run while typing. Agent first message carries the open note as context when one is open; '/' focuses the column input while the column is open.

verification.command: npm run typecheck && npm run build && npm test
verification.expected: Gates green. Playwright: mode changes placeholder; semantic Enter -> column rows -> click opens note; web Enter (mocked) -> results + save; agent Enter -> chat in column; column open hides search bar + left-docks reader; close restores; no-mode search unchanged

features.context_note: Reader geometry engine (applyGeom/localStorage akasha-reader) must not fight the forced-left state: use a CSS class override on the docked state only, never mutate saved geometry. Menubar click-outside gotcha unchanged.
features.evidence: typecheck+build+121 tests green. Playwright on real vault: mode changes placeholder+accent; no dropdown while a mode is active; semantic Enter -> column with 11 results, click swaps note, column persists; search bar hides and reader force-docks LEFT (needed !important over the geometry engine's inline inset); close restores search bar + right reader; web Enter (mocked) -> column results with save button; agent Enter -> real session, note context notice, user bubble, stop button; '/' targets column input; Escape order includes the column; shortcut suppression verified while focused. Fixed en route: MCP snippet line-number/@@ decoration stripped. NOTE: test overwrote the stored Exa key with a fake; cleared to null — Felo must re-enter his key if he had one
features.verified_by: gates exit 0 + playwright full-flow verification

features.behavior: Per-note research questions: every opened note ends with a 'research questions' action (near the related-notes section) that generates 3-5 template questions from THAT note (its unresolved phantom links first, then title/topic templates; 3 for short notes, up to 5). Generation is local and free. Clicking a question (or the user's own follow-up) runs it as a web research query through the existing consent+key gates and opens the results in the research column with the query in its follow-up input. The old global gap-suggestions UI is gone (server /api/gaps stays for agent context).

verification.command: npm test && npm run typecheck && npm run build
verification.expected: topology tests: note-scoped questions from a fixture (phantom-linked note -> phantom queries first; short note -> 3; rich note -> 5; no phantoms -> title templates). Playwright: button renders under a note, click shows questions, executing one opens the column via the web path

features.context_note: Questions are templates, no LLM. Execution path = the same consent-gated /api/research; no key -> actionable message pointing at Tools -> Integrations.
features.evidence: 124/124 tests + typecheck + build green. noteQuestions: note's own phantom links first (path noise filtered), slug titles humanized, 3 for short notes / 5 for rich. Playwright on real vault: button renders after related section, click generates 5 questions, executing one opens the research column via the consent-gated web path (no-key case shows the actionable Tools -> Integrations message, reader left-docked). Works with no mode button lit. Docs updated to the search-first model

features.behavior: Exa deep research: the adapter supports deep mode returning a synthesized answer with grounded citations (type:'deep' + outputSchema:{type:'text'}; response.output.content + citations) alongside the source results. Per-note research questions execute as deep research; manual column queries stay fast with a 'deep research' toggle in the column input row. The column renders the answer paragraph above a Sources list; save-as-note captures the synthesized answer WITH its citations into the vault. Deep runs show a 'researching deeply, takes a while' state.

The search-field agent query is typed into the TUI after boot. node-pty missing -> clean 503 with guidance. The sandboxed bridge remains as the headless engine (LLM questions, model selector). features.behavior: Solaris config persists an optional `embedModel` (a qmd `hf:org/repo/file.gguf` reference; null = qmd default). The Runner accepts an optional env map and realRunner merges it into the child process env. The qmd maintenance embed spawn carries `QMD_EMBED_MODEL` when a model is set and adds `-f` (force) when requested, so a later re-embed rebuilds vectors with the chosen model.

Runner env is additive (4th optional param), backward-compatible with fake runners. QMD_EMBED_MODEL format = hf:Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B-GGUF/Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B-Q8_0.gguf.

The re-embed (qmd embed -f) button remains.

features.behavior: New server/integrations/qmd-vectors.ts behind the qmd adapter: opens ~/.cache/qmd/index.sqlite READ-ONLY (better-sqlite3 + the sqlite-vec loadable extension). API: docVector(path) (mean-pool a note's chunk vectors), knn(vector,k) (KNN via `embedding MATCH ? ORDER BY distance` on the vectors_vec vec0 table), allDocVectors() (batch). The embedding dimension is READ FROM the vectors_vec schema (`float[N]`), never hardcoded to 768. Schema-version guard: probe vectors_vec/content_vectors shape on open; on unexpected/absent schema, disable the semantic layer and report a clear status instead of crashing. Injectable db path for tests (tiny fixture index). NOT a daemon; live vsearch stays as-is.

verification.expected: new server/integrations/qmd-vectors.test.ts passes: KNN over a fixture index returns the expected neighbors; dimension is read from the schema (a 1024-dim fixture is not treated as 768); a malformed/absent index disables the layer (no throw).

features.context_note: Adds native deps (better-sqlite3 + sqlite-vec) — the plan chose direct-read over the SDK/daemon (§4). Quarantine ALL sqlite coupling here behind a schema guard + graceful 'semantic layer unavailable' fallback (constraint §2). Read dim from vectors_vec `float[N]`, never hardcode 768 (embeddinggemma today; a model switch changes it). Join content_vectors(hash,seq,pos,model,total_chunks) -> documents(id,path,title,hash,active). Read-only open; no egress.
features.evidence: npm test: 136/136 pass incl 4 new qmd-vectors tests (KNN neighbors, dim read from float[N] schema incl 1024-dim, absent+malformed index disable the layer without throwing). Real index smoke: dim=768, 8671 in-vault docs, KNN semantically coherent.
features.verified_by: npm test

features.behavior: Semantic edge set: doc-level KNN over qmd-vectors (F030) producing data/semantic.json (gitignored, like graph.json). Edge hygiene to avoid a hairball: MUTUAL-KNN only (A in topK(B) AND B in topK(A)), cosine >= ~0.5 threshold, K capped 6-8. Store {source,target,score} where source/target are note ids that MATCH graph.json node ids. Incremental: keyed by documents.hash; on rescan recompute only changed notes' neighborhoods. Exposed via an endpoint/build step.

verification.expected: semantic-edges test: edge set is symmetric (mutual-KNN), thresholded (every score >= threshold), bounded (<= ~K*N/2); a spot-check confirms known-related-but-unlinked fixture notes are connected.

features.context_note: Mutual-KNN + cosine>=~0.5 + K 6-8 is the anti-hairball rule (do NOT skip). {source,target} ids must equal graph.json node ids (note path/basename) so the frontend maps edges to nodes. data/semantic.json is gitignored runtime data like graph.json. Incremental by documents.hash.
features.evidence: npm test 138/138 (2 new semantic tests: thresholded, bounded<=K*N/2, undirected-deduped, MUTUAL-only rejection of one-way NN, cluster spot-check, non-graph nodes excluded). Live /api/semantic on real vault: 2078 mutual-KNN edges (dim 768, thr 0.5), cold build 14.6s -> cached 14ms in data/semantic.json (gitignored, keyed by graph fingerprint). Impl in new server/integrations/semantic.ts (cooperative O(n^2) cosine) + /api/semantic in app.ts; qmd-vectors hardened to lazy-require native deps.
features.verified_by: npm test

features.behavior: Arrangement modes: a selector next to `group by` in View with Links (default), Semantic, Hybrid. Feed the chosen edge array into the 3d-force-graph sim (one force mechanism, three edge sets). Hybrid weights semantic edges ~0.3-0.5x link strength (links=skeleton, semantics fill gaps + rescue orphans). Semantic edges are ALWAYS rendered in a distinct faint/dashed style; a toggle hides the lines while keeping their physical pull. Cache node positions PER ARRANGEMENT (extend /api/layout + cachedPositions) and tween on switch instead of re-simulating live. Choice persists in localStorage (akasha-*).

verification.expected: typecheck clean; agent-browser: each mode (Links/Semantic/Hybrid) yields a stable, distinct layout; switching tweens and reuses cached positions; semantic edges draw dashed; the hide-lines toggle keeps the pull; frame budget acceptable at full vault (~4k nodes).

features.context_note: Default = Links (don't surprise on first load — decided §4). 'Drawn != simulated': semantic edges always dashed even when only their pull is used; a toggle hides lines but keeps physics. Cache positions per arrangement + tween (reuse the warmup-then-freeze pattern). Perf: +10-15k edges at 4k nodes -> server precompute + freeze; Hybrid is heaviest, measure. Follow the existing View-menu control + localStorage pattern.
features.evidence: typecheck clean; 138 tests pass. agent-browser at full vault (4014 nodes): Links(4997)/Semantic(2078)/Hybrid(7075) sim link sets each yield a distinct stable layout; semantic edges render dashed (LineDashedMaterial buffer); hide-lines toggle keeps sim link count (pull preserved), only rendering hidden; per-arrangement positions cached to data/layout-<mode>.json via /api/layout?arrangement= and restored fast (warmup 0) on re-switch; choice + toggle persist in akasha-* localStorage. Transition is a cache-seeded quick-settle (not a pixel-lerp).
features.verified_by: npm run typecheck + agent-browser

features.behavior: Semantic grouping/color: a `group by: semantic cluster` option that runs community detection (e.g. label propagation) on the Phase-1 mutual-KNN graph. Coloring works under ANY arrangement (color axis is orthogonal to layout axis). Optional cluster labels via one LLM pass (name each community from its top terms), behind OpenRouter and optional.

verification.expected: typecheck clean; agent-browser: 'semantic cluster' appears in group-by; clusters are stable across reloads (seeded/deterministic); coloring is independent of the chosen arrangement (works under Links/Semantic/Hybrid).

features.context_note: Color axis is ORTHOGONAL to layout (Phase 2): semantic-cluster coloring must work under any arrangement. Seed label propagation on the Phase-1 mutual-KNN graph so clusters are stable across reloads. LLM cluster labels are OPTIONAL and behind OpenRouter (no egress without a key). Follow the existing `group by` (folder/tag) pattern.
features.evidence: typecheck clean. agent-browser: 'semantic cluster' added to group-by; selecting it recolors nodes by deterministic label propagation over the F031 mutual-KNN edges (seeded per-node label + fixed order + lexicographic tie-break, no randomness). Clusters IDENTICAL across a full page reload (same legend signature: #agencia29/#dev25/#podcast20/... other1235/unclustered2494). Coloring is orthogonal to layout: verified under both Links and Hybrid arrangements. Cluster names derive from top tag/pillar (free heuristic); LLM labeling left as the optional future enhancement per spec.
features.verified_by: npm run typecheck + agent-browser

features.behavior: Orphan link suggestions: upgrade /api/gaps so each orphan/sparse node surfaces its top semantic neighbor(s) as 'link these?'. PREVIEW-THEN-CONFIRM one-click insert appends a [[wiki]] link ONLY through server/integrations/write.ts (journaled to data/changes.jsonl). Never auto-writes. Bounded to orphans (no batch cost); works even without Phase 1 by using live vsearch.

verification.expected: gaps-suggest test: a suggestion resolves to a real in-vault node; insertion goes ONLY through the guarded writer (write.ts) and is journaled; an unconfirmed suggestion writes nothing; the path-traversal + write guards stay green.

features.context_note: PREVIEW-THEN-CONFIRM, never auto-write (decided — it mutates the vault). Insertion MUST go through server/integrations/write.ts (the SINGLE sanctioned write path) — never add a second writer (AGENTS.md). Journaled to data/changes.jsonl. Works without Phase 1 (live vsearch), so depends_on is empty. Keep the path-traversal + consent tests green (release-blocking).
features.evidence: npm test 145/145 incl 8 new write.test.ts cases: guardedAppendLink appends [[wiki]] + journals; idempotent (no dup, no journal); 404 on missing note; confinement rejects traversal (400); POST /api/gaps/link 403 without token, appends+journals with token; GET /api/gaps writes nothing (read-only). Server enriches orphan gap suggestions with their top neighbor from the cached edges (readSemanticCache, never triggers a build). agent-browser: opening orphan notas/vibe-docs.md shows a preview-then-confirm card 'LINK SUGGESTION: [[ui-libraries]] 72% similar / add this link' (998 orphans have a neighbor). Live vault mutation intentionally NOT triggered in test; write path proven by unit tests.
features.verified_by: npm test + agent-browser

features.behavior: Passage-level reader: on a SEMANTIC hit, scroll to and highlight the matched chunk in the reader instead of opening the note at the top, using content_vectors.pos (char offset) or the `@@ -n,n @@` line range already in vsearch snippets. Normal (node-click) opens are unchanged.

features.context_note: Only for SEMANTIC hits; normal opens must stay unchanged. Use content_vectors.pos (char offset) or the vsearch `@@ -n,n @@` line range. Keep openReader's DOMPurify sanitize intact when injecting the highlight.
features.evidence: typecheck clean; 145 tests pass. agent-browser: a semantic-search hit (claude-md-guide) opens the reader scrolled to + highlighting the matched passage ('Note: this post is also applicable to AGENTS.md...'), which spans <em>/<code> boundaries; painted via the CSS Custom Highlight API (no DOM mutation -> DOMPurify intact). Wired for all snippet-bearing semantic paths (semantic search, related notes).

features.behavior: During a live voice session, a subtle horizontal waveform renders on a canvas directly above #brand-stats, driven by real mic input (human) and agent playback audio (agent) tapped from the existing voice graph in web/src/voice.ts. One color at a time: agent speaking uses the active theme --accent, human speaking uses its runtime HSL complement, idle (neither) renders a flat dim low-opacity line. Mounts on voice start, unmounts on voice end so cost is zero when voice is off. Render loop is a single requestAnimationFrame, allocation-free, fftSize 512, smoothing 0.85.

verification.expected: tsc --noEmit clean; scanner + server tests pass (regression guard). Behavioral (deferred review): AE1 turn-taking color, AE4 canvas absent when voice off; verify with npm run dev

features.context_note: Two Analysers: micAnalyser on captureCtx tapped parallel to the worklet (srcNode -> micAnalyser -> existing muted gain, alongside srcNode -> worklet), agentAnalyser on playCtx as a single persistent bus every BufferSource routes through before destination (node -> agentAnalyser -> playCtx.destination). Expose both to main.ts. Complement color = HSL rotate 180 of --accent. Speaking detection by RMS with hysteresis (tune at impl). See plan 004 KTD1-5 + audio tap mermaid.

features.behavior: The #brand-stats footer splits into two independent halves. Left half mirrors the content/reader panel: word count when a real note is open in the reader, else reverts to notes count. Right half mirrors the research panel: matches for semantic, results for web, percent during ingest, words for article/document, else reverts to links count. While a voice session is live, both halves override: left shows provider + voice name, right shows a mm:ss session timer; on voice end reverts to whatever reader/research state dictates. Each half reverts the moment its panel closes.

Reactive to live drag via the existing MutationObserver-driven relayout(). Right-side collapse behavior is unchanged (regression-safe).

In layoutTopbar() main.ts:3787-3828 add a left-collision check: when leftDocked and leftPanelW + PAD > vw/2 - groupW/2, toggle a new menu-jumped-right class plus search-stacked; otherwise preserve menu-centered. Add CSS #topbar.menu-jumped-right #nav-group near style.css:253-268 to position nav-group at left = --btn-left-inset + 18px. See plan 005 KTD1-3 + state diagram.

features.behavior: layoutTopbar() resolves a single topbar state (ROW / STACKED / RAIL) from available width (viewport minus docked panel widths) and the crowding side. The left panel continuously slides the menu+search rightward as a group (via a --left-inset CSS var mirroring the existing --right-inset), replacing F039's centered-then-jump. RAIL engages when available width drops below the horizontal topbar's minimum. F039's menu-jumped-right class and its collision branch are removed; the search-stacked (STACKED) intermediate is kept for right-panel crowding; F039's z-index bump (topbar 25) is retained.

verification.expected: tsc --noEmit clean; scanner + server tests pass (regression guard). Behavioral (deferred review): AE2 left continuous-slide (no jump), AE3 right stack regression, AE6 reactive to live drag; verify with npm run dev

features.context_note: Extend layoutTopbar() at main.ts:~4079 to compute availableWidth = vw - leftPanelW - rightPanelW and resolve state in {row, stacked, rail}. Set --left-inset = leftPanelW consumed by #nav-group and #search-wrap (mirror --right-inset at style.css:~246) so the chrome slides right as a unit. Remove the menu-jumped-right class (added by F039) and its collision branch; keep search-stacked for the right-side wrap. See plan 006 KTD1-3 + state diagram.
features.evidence: npm run typecheck clean; npm test 161/161; npm run build green. Behavioral AEs deferred to manual npm run dev review (rail UI is highly visual; expect iteration on flyout positioning and thresholds).
features.verified_by: harness-progress verify (npm run typecheck && npm test && npm run build, exit 0)

features.behavior: RAIL state renders a ~52px vertical icon rail pinned to the right edge: logo at top, then search/mode/voice icons, then File/Layers/View/Tools/Help at the bottom. The horizontal #nav-group and #search-wrap hide in RAIL. Tapping a menu icon opens its existing .menu dropdown repositioned beside the rail; tapping search opens a flyout hosting the existing #search input, #modes, and #voice-toggle with #search-results rendering inline. Closing a flyout returns to the rail.

verification.expected: tsc --noEmit clean; scanner + server tests pass. Behavioral (deferred review): AE1 rail on narrow viewport, AE4 flyouts (menu beside rail, search inline results), click-outside + menu-stays-open gotcha; verify with npm run dev

features.context_note: Add a #topbar-rail element to index.html (logo + search/mode/voice icons + divider + File/Layers/View/Tools/Help icons), hidden by default, shown via the topbar-rail class toggled by F040. Hide #nav-group/#search-wrap in rail state. Wire each rail menu icon to open its existing .menu dropdown repositioned left of the rail (reuse the global click-outside handler; preserve the click-inside-.menu stays-open gotcha). Search icon opens a flyout hosting #search/#modes/#voice-toggle with #search-results inline. See plan 006 KTD4.
features.evidence: npm run typecheck clean; npm test 161/161; npm run build green. Behavioral AEs deferred to manual npm run dev review (rail UI is highly visual; expect iteration on flyout positioning and thresholds).

features.behavior: While RAIL is active, docked panels shrink by the rail's width (~52px) and restore when RAIL disengages. A --rail-inset CSS var (52px in RAIL, 0 otherwise) feeds the existing panel drag clamps and dock max-width so neither panel can extend under the rail. A user-widened panel is clamped on RAIL engage and restores on disengage. Reactive to live drag.

verification.expected: tsc --noEmit clean; scanner + server tests pass. Behavioral (deferred review): panel narrows ~52px in RAIL, restores on disengage, both-panels case clamps, live drag updates; verify with npm run dev

features.context_note: F040's state resolver sets --rail-inset (52px in RAIL, 0 otherwise) on the root. Feed it into the panel drag clamps in applyGeom() (main.ts:~2281) and applyRGeom() (main.ts:~3890) and the dock max-width so panels cannot underlap the rail. Mirror the --btn-left-inset/--btn-right-inset clamp pattern. See plan 006 KTD5.
